summary: Created a separate Agentic RAG direction-build ledger after Chairman corrected the optimization target from theoretical best to fast measurable business fit.
Generated a deterministic four-asset, 72-hour, ten-second synthetic HVAC corpus with documents, configuration, point aliases, DuckDB, Parquet and hidden event truth.
Frozen 52 candidate-neutral cases and recomputed all numerical gold truth from DuckDB; focused TDD verification passed 6 tests.
Started the fast single-chat direction build against the new measured corpus.
Recorded the no-silent-downgrade rule and the authorized Codex Switch Responses API as the required model-backed path; the offline shell cannot satisfy intelligence acceptance.
The authorized Responses API completed a grounded knowledge-plus-data answer, a bounded conversational follow-up, structured tables/charts/citations and a 320px completed-state journey.

Completed the lightweight Haystack plus DuckDB single-chat direction build with read-only tool budgets, outer deadline, source status, model-backed routing and fail-closed evidence boundaries; AR-05 candidate measurement is active.
Kept real runtime databases blocked while allowing only the exact signed synthetic 7.1 MB DuckDB trial snapshot; raw CSV and Parquet stay generator-reproducible and out of Git.
The final post-refinement code rereview reported Critical 0 and Important 0.
Fresh verification passed Ruff, formatting, 196 tests with five documented optional skips, release guard, isolated wheel build and the final two-turn real-model desktop/320px browser journey.
Retained 52/52 real-model responses with zero execution failures; strict baseline measured 46.2% behavior, 42.3% tool contract and 34.1% evidence contract.

TDD added typed DuckDB snapshot inspection for data quality, control events and alarms plus governed metric-extreme windows; deterministic safety and default-snapshot routing were tightened without increasing Agent budget.
Final 52/52 real-model run had zero execution failures and measured 82.7% behavior, 82.7% tool contract and 81.8% evidence contract; independent HVAC adjudication accepted 49/52 responses.

Official GitHub/API evidence retained Haystack plus DuckDB as the current lightweight delivery, Wren as a future NL2SQL component, DB-GPT as a conditional WSL2 challenger and RAGFlow as a deferred dedicated deployment; the project business footprint is about 51 MB, not tens of GB.
The live benchmark now aborts after two consecutive provider failures and resumes only after benchmark, candidate, endpoint, model, revision and provenance validation.
TDD fixed exclusive event-end semantics, the 60-second formal compressor mismatch boundary, short observations, A311 alarm filtering and common fan/EEV event-name aliases.
The real-model desktop and mobile single-Chat journey passed with Markdown, table, chart, source cards, human-readable project/data trace and a history-aware kWh follow-up.
Final v29 executed 52/52 real-model requests with zero execution failures and passed 52/52 behavior, 52/52 tool and 44/44 evidence contracts.
Independent HVAC adjudication accepted 52/52 v29 answers and bound the review to raw-result SHA bb09feb546caea8c17c44e4a8572474eec08a9918d456b01763fff4a03abf5c0.
Measured business source/configuration/data footprint is about 51 MB and the release wheel about 4.6 MB; RAGFlow's 50 GB allowance is not a Project Copilot requirement.

TDD and independent review closed directional fan aliases, immutable manifest citations, history relevance, evidence-before-synthesis, same-intent SQL recovery, provider transport classification, subset-resume truncation, frozen-sensor aliases and unnecessary root-cause clarification.
Final v35 executed 52/52 real-model requests with zero execution failures and passed 52/52 behavior, 52/52 tool and 44/44 evidence contracts under candidate revision content-2388bafba3c6bc6f.

Independent HVAC review accepted 52/52 v35 answers, including D15 and C07, and the formal adjudication bound all 52 decisions to raw-result SHA f17bf6a25f333570ebb73daeb3c43bed13069438c19c32b5bf27a1208285fbca.
The final wheel is 4,610,767 bytes, retains the compact DuckDB corpus, and both wheel and sdist exclude benchmark hidden_truth; pip check, pip-audit and LicenseCheck passed.
Fresh verification passed Ruff, formatting and 297 tests with five documented optional skips; commit, non-force push and GitHub CI remain.

The Chairman broadened the product from HVAC-only to a local-private general project brain, required a fixed composer, removal of the legacy Workbench user journey, an animated auditable project map and strict separation of real local data from the public repository.

The Chairman required conclusions, rankings and key numbers to remain visually dominant while provenance, statistical scope and limitations are retained as smaller secondary text to reduce cognitive load.
Resumed the bounded worker under the rule that every requirement begins with current Web and GitHub research and mature components are integrated before custom implementation.

The Chairman retired the complex management workflow: file upload must sit beside the single Chat composer, ingestion and indexing are automatic, and the exact original filename is the primary citation identity familiar to engineers.
TDD and browser acceptance proved bounded message scrolling, a fixed composer, Chat-local uploads, compact evidence/limitations, progressive citations and the retired legacy Workbench user journey.
Vendored Cytoscape.js 3.34.0 with MIT notice; large projects render category counts and animate only auditable cited files, while small projects can render all file nodes.

Repository-external read-only catalog indexed 196 of 198 discovered project files, preserved original filenames, excluded secrets and OnionQuant runtime paths, and kept private runtime outside the public worktree.

Real-model browser acceptance returned one concise answer, one original-filename citation and one highlighted graph node for AGENTIC_RAG_TASK_LEDGER.json while keeping the composer fixed; Office parsing, final verification, review, docs, commit and push remain.

A D-drive documents environment with local all-MiniLM-L6-v2 tokenizer and local Docling layout artifacts passed the real PDF+DOCX parse, chunk, index, page-citation and restart integration test with remote model access disabled.
The frozen baseline plus conversation queue, evidence workbench and engineering canvas routes became runnable over one shared model, corpus, read-only tool and original-filename evidence contract.

The frozen 14-case real-model workload completed 14/14 requests with zero execution failures but passed only 1/14 automatic hard gates; the result is retained as failure evidence rather than weakened or replaced.

UI review scored baseline 2/5, conversation 3/5, evidence 4/5 and canvas 4/5; independent HVAC review scored pass 0, partial 6, fail 8 (21.4/100); final code rereview reported Critical 0 and Important 0.

Systematic debugging closed the percentage-point parser overlap, restored passage-level evidence for short documents, regenerated documents locks with pytz, and began fresh full tests, browser acceptance, release guard and packaging verification.

Ruff, 391 collected Python tests, the 9-pass current-UI browser suite, release guard, wheel/sdist content checks, wheel install smoke, strict dependency audit and license compatibility passed. The opt-in real-model browser query failed numeric grounding consistently with the retained 1/14 complex benchmark; commit and push remain.

Independent final staged review reported Critical 0 and Important 0. Commit 3151a02, including the four architecture routes, final benchmark failure evidence, deployment handoff, tests and public-safety controls, was pushed without force to origin/codex/agentic-rag-bakeoff.

GitHub Actions run 29615856293 was retained as failed evidence after Ubuntu inherited UTC for standalone snapshot queries and Windows lacked ZoneInfo IANA data. TDD now requires an explicit project timezone, the CPython-maintained tzdata package replaces the direct pytz dependency, all lock chains are regenerated, and release closure waits for a green replacement run.

The full Python suite, deterministic 23/23 offline evaluation, browser acceptance, Ruff, release guard and dependency consistency exited successfully. Independent portability review reported Critical 0, Important 0 and Minor 0 after correcting the stale packaging assertion; a green replacement GitHub Actions run remains the only release blocker.

GitHub Actions run 29617892095 passed Ubuntu and Windows test/evaluation/release-guard jobs, package/SBOM and wheel smoke, browser acceptance, offline document parsing and Gitleaks for commit b5eed1e. The four-version architecture delivery is closed; the retained 1/14 intelligence gate remains a separate future bounded backend-quality objective.
